rulings.Case Title: FLORENCE KONG VS. MARIANO HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2024 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SRF RECOVERY, KWAN WO IRONWORKS, INC., FLORENCE KONG DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED at this time. Demurrer to causes of action for Breach of Contract, Quantum Meruit, and Account Stated based on statute of limitations in overruled. These causes of action relate back to the filing of the original complaint. For the purposes of the demurrer will consider the Cross-Complaint's allegations regarding the fence as true. There appears to be a factual dispute between the parties as to the location, timing and erection of various types of fences. This factual dispute cannot be addressed on a demurrer. Demurrer to the causes of action for Public Nuisance, Private Nuisance, Premises Liability, Trespass, Declaratory and Injunctive Relief is OVERRULED. Claims against third-parties are properly brought in this cross-complaint. Alternatively, the Court will sustain the demurrer to these causes of action per the cross-defendant's request in this case, vacate the dismissal in case 594093 and will entertain a motion to consolidate. If parties wish to proceed with the alternative ruling as stated above, the parties shall so advice the Court at the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAUREN GEREMIA VS. SIDE, INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION D/B/A INDIGO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT BILQUES SMITH , AN INDIVIDUAL, SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained as to cause of action for negligent misrepresentation that allege this claim with requisite particularity. Additionally, Plaintiff shall clarify the meaning of the word "they" (plural) as used in paragraph 45 of FAC. The Court notes that Plaintiff is referred to as "she" (singular) elsewhere in the Complaint (e.g. paragraph 4). The Court further notes that this is not the only uncertainty in the FAC. See paragraph 5 "In this case Defendant Vanguard and Defendant Costa failed to provide the proper disclosures and also failed to disclose a serious defects at the Property." Demurrer to the causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and negligence is OVERRULED. All elements of these causes of action, including duty, as sufficiently stated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: POLK STREET ASSOCIATES VS. BERBER GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT BERBER GROUP, LLC DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Moving party fails to state any authority for the proposition that allegations of compliance with the fictitious business name is required to state a cause of action for breach of contract (this is no longer an unlawful detainer action). The Complaint alleges that the lease is written and that Defendant breached the lease for the periods after the filing of the prior action "CGC-21-590069," i.e. 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: POLK STREET ASSOCIATES VS. BERBER GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT BERBER GROUP, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a). The Notice of motion in relevant part appears to be incomplete and states: "Strikeout any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading, that multiple paragraphs." Moving party failed to show which subsection of CRC 2.112 Plaintiff failed to comply with. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISE, INC. VS. MARY BONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT MARY BONG, CHARLES CHUA, CHARLESTON CHUA, KINGSTON CHUA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to September 20, 2024. No later than September 3, 2024 Plaintiff to file a Proof of Service for Amended Summons and Complaint issued on July 10, 2024. Before this case proceeds with further motions it must be clarified whether this action proceeds as a civil action or as unlawful detainer. If no proof of service is filed by September 3, 2024, the case will proceed as a civil case and service of amended summons/filing of the proof of service without further order of the Court will not result in an automatic conversion of the case. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
